evals.name: unilever-canonical
evals.prompt: Build me a Design System from https://www.unilever.com so I can author HTML slides in their visual language. Save the result to ./unilever-PPT-Design-System.md
evals.expected_output: A complete DS markdown that mirrors the structural shape of the canonical reference DS: §1 Design Philosophy with Constraints/Freedom split, §2 Colour Tokens (token names invariant, hex from extraction), §3 Typography with type scale + readability floor, §4 Logo with SVG <symbol> + currentColor, §5 Slide Architecture incl. full Fit Contract, §6 11 slide types, §7 Component Library (Panel/Showcase/Item/Stat/Callout/Table/Charts/Tabs/Timeline), §8 Imagery, §9 Navigation, §10 Mobile incl. inline-flex trap + flip-card mobile fix, §11 Animation, §12 Layout Rules, §13 ~40-item Checklist incl. 375px verification.

evals.name: stripe-engineering-aesthetic
evals.prompt: Generate a Design System from https://stripe.com — for product/SaaS slide decks. We want the engineering-clean aesthetic.
evals.expected_output: A DS reading as engineering-clean (purple/blurple accent, Sohne or Inter family, dense grid). Engineering DNA section preserved (fit contract, mobile inline-flex trap, flip-card mobile fix, logo as SVG symbol). Brand-variable parts adapted to Stripe.

evals.name: minimal-monochrome-brand
evals.prompt: Build a design system from https://www.apple.com — for executive briefing decks. I want minimal, monochrome, type-led.
evals.expected_output: A DS where the philosophy paragraph reads minimal/monochrome, palette is restrained, but the same engineering DNA is preserved.

evals.name: pg-end-to-end-with-deck
evals.prompt: Build a Design System from https://www.pg.com (Procter & Gamble) for an internal corporate-update deck. After the DS is generated, write 5–6 slides of original copy yourself — about a fictional 'P&G China Q2 brand performance' update — and produce a complete standalone HTML file (1280×720, single-file, opens in a browser) that exercises the DS: cover slide + dark-bg slide showing the logo in white + light-bg slide showing the logo in brand-dark + a two-column comparison + a chart slide + a closing pull-quote. Save the DS to ./pg-PPT-Design-System.md, save the deck to ./pg-deck.html, and save the workspace under ./.web-to-ds-workspace/pg-<ts>/.
evals.expected_output: Two artifacts: a DS markdown matching template structure with P&G-specific palette (likely a corporate blue plus warm neutrals) + an HTML deck file that renders without console errors at native 1280×720. The deck must demonstrate logo flip (W on dark, L on light), token-only colors, and at 375px width must collapse to single-column without horizontal scroll.
manual_review: Open ./pg-deck.html at native 1280×720 — does it look on-brand for P&G?
Resize browser to 375px — do slides stack vertically with no horizontal scroll?
Cycle through all slides — does the logo flip cleanly from white-on-dark to brand-on-light?
Is the slide copy legible, is the cover commanding, is there visible bottom breathing room on every content slide?
